message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
I already told you I can’t pay right now, and I’m not giving more details.
Understood. I’ll stop the assessment here and note that you cannot pay right now and do not want to provide more details in this channel.

Borrower stated they cannot pay right now and do not want to provide more details in this channel; this is a clear boundary signal and second refusal after prior capacity discussion. Case state shows hardship signals job_loss and student, with can_pay_later=true and expected_date 2026-04-20, but those later-capacity facts were not newly provided in this turn. Stop-contact flag is present in case state, and the borrower\u2019s latest message indicates no further assessment in this channel. Already asked: initial disclosure/debt-establishment reply requested account-reference last four digits; borrower did not complete transcript-supported verification in the visible exchange. Priority for Agent 2: do not continue assessment, do not re-ask capacity or verification in this channel, and only review policy-allowed next steps consistent with the borrower\u2019s no-more-details boundary and existing stop-contact status."}

I’m unemployed right now, so stop asking—I’m not committing to any payment.

Understood. I’ve recorded that you’re unemployed, not committing to any payment, and that you want contact to stop. I will not continue this discussion in this channel. For this account ending ****4831, I cannot offer any extension beyond 30 days under current policy. If a lender-approved hardship or other approved option is available without further discussion, that can be handled through the recorded next-step process.

{"from_stage": "RESOLUTION", "to_stage": "FINAL_NOTICE", "summary": "Conversation already in progress; identity disclosure and recording/logging disclosure were already completed earlier and should not be repeated. Borrower\u2019s latest stated position is that they are unemployed, are not committing to any payment, and want the asking/contact to stop in this channel. Hardship signals include job loss/unemployment and student status. Agent 2 checked available data and policy: current policy says no extensions beyond 30 days, and reduced closure and payment-plan paths require policy-compliant approval; no concrete approved option was presented in this turn. Objection raised was inability/unwillingness to commit and refusal to continue discussing details. Stop-contact request is present in case state and was reaffirmed by the borrower. Agent 2 told the borrower the unemployment hardship and no-commitment position were recorded, that discussion would not continue in this channel, and that only any lender-approved hardship or other approved option should be handled through the recorded next-step process. Agent 3 should continue naturally without restarting, avoid repeating disclosures, verification, assessment questions, or rejected payment-commitment asks, and must honor the stop-contact instruction unless a documented compliant exception exists."}
